rationale: The user identified the HIL setup paragraphs as report-like and insufficiently rigorous. First iteration focuses on architecture, delay/noise units, and measured physical quantities.
The HIL paragraph converts normalized noise to physical units. The manuscript's parameter table uses other reference scales, so the HIL-specific input normalization scales must be explicit.
The platform paragraph should describe the closed-loop control path precisely: local observation, actor inference, normalized action, terminal PN/action mapping, dynamics propagation.

note: Replaced informal “明显控制振荡” wording with “可辨识的控制振荡” in the 6x6 HIL kinematic analysis.
Removed the unsupported intensity adverb 显著 before the numeric preference value and softened an absolute inability claim into a time-bounded observation.
Replaced a qualitative significance claim with an engineering spatial-safety-margin interpretation tied to the reported minimum interceptor distance.
Replaced qualitative high-threat/high-preference summary wording with numeric ranges from the three representative HIL cases.
Replaced the colloquial role-mis-switching claim with a numeric task-preference evolution statement from 0.85 to 0.97.
Replaced abstract 8x8 distance-window wording with a 50x damage-radius margin and the D3 target-switch timing.
Replaced abstract penetration-advantage wording with a conclusion tied to early allocation-failure ratio and closest-interceptor safety distance.
Removed the undefined confidence framing and used the main-attack preference increase from 0.75 to 0.97 as direct evidence.
Replaced the colloquial 越来越难以 trend wording with a time-bounded statement that interceptors did not restore an effective main-direction encirclement before impact.
Replaced qualitative high/increasing main-attack preference wording with the measured increase from 0.84 to 0.96 under high threat and target-selection ratio.
Bounded 10x10 HIL claims to the maximum tested scale instead of implying extrapolation to untested higher scales.
Replaced qualitative effective-damage wording with a statement tied to the 242.41 m closest-interceptor distance staying outside the 5 m damage radius.
Replaced qualitative ablation-drop wording with exact success rates 17.5%, 68.0%, and 93.5% for the threat-margin channel removal.
Replaced speculative baseline mechanism explanation with direct success-rate range comparison across methods.
Replaced qualitative stable-cooperation wording with the direct lower bound that penetration success is not below 98.5% across tested scales.
Scoped the miss-distance conclusion to the complete method and inserted its 4.74--4.80 m average miss-distance range.
Aligned conclusion item 4 with the quantified HIL and Monte Carlo evidence already introduced in the simulation section.
Removed duplicate MC success-rate lower-bound statement after the MC table and converted it into a transition to ablation analysis.
Replaced direct qualitative mechanism degradation wording with a bounded statement that the success-rate decrease corresponds to weakened decoy and main-channel maintenance.
Replaced the strong key-factor summary claim with compact numerical ablation evidence from the prior-channel table.
Removed the key-factor claim from conclusion item 4 and replaced it with the two ablation success-rate ranges.
Bounded remaining different-scale phrasing to the three tested scales in the Monte Carlo introduction and simulation summary.
Improved conclusion intro tone and bounded the performance claim to the three tested attack-defense scales and local perturbation conditions.
Replaced broad improve/enhance wording in the conclusion opening with the 98.5% success-rate lower bound and HIL task-differentiation evidence.
Softened conclusion item 3 from an absolute effective-improvement claim to a bounded helps-improve convergence-level statement.
Bounded 8x8 and 10x10 trajectory interpretation to representative-case observations instead of broad scale-generalization claims.
Recast the 6x6 HIL trajectory conclusion as a representative-case observation rather than a general capability claim.
Recast 10x10 HIL role-maintenance and closed-loop feasibility statements as representative-case evidence rather than general ability claims.
Recast the 8x8 role-preference conclusion as a representative-episode observation instead of a general result claim.
Replaced 从机理层面展示 with 呈现 to keep the three HIL representative episodes within a case-observation evidence boundary.
Separated representative HIL case observations from Monte Carlo statistical support in the simulation summary opening sentence.

name: ablation_to_role_switching_causal_interpretation_moderation
note: Moderated ablation interpretation from directly supporting/affecting role switching to statistical association with reduced role-switching capability.

name: ablation_interpretation_limited_to_success_rate_sensitivity
note: Removed unmeasured role-switching capability claims from ablation interpretation and limited conclusions to success-rate sensitivity to risk and team-benefit information.

name: conclusion_ablation_condition_terminology_alignment
note: Qualified the conclusion ablation statement as server-side local-perturbation ablation to match the simulation section table caption and setup.

name: simulation_section_summary_compression
note: Compressed the simulation-section summary to reduce repeated numerical listing while preserving HIL, Monte-Carlo, and ablation evidence.

name: conclusion_wording_and_punctuation_polish
note: Polished conclusion lead sentence around RTA-MAPPO spacing/wording and replaced half-width semicolons in conclusion items.

name: statistical_table_caption_compression
note: Shortened Monte-Carlo and ablation table captions while preserving server-side local-perturbation and per-case episode-count information.

name: abstract_result_scope_terminology_alignment
note: Aligned abstract result scope with the simulation/conclusion wording by using three offensive-defense scales and specifying that Monte-Carlo success rate applies across the three scales.

name: mechanism_wording_moderation_in_abstract_contribution_and_conclusion
note: Changed “drive formation” wording to “guide formation” in abstract, contribution, and conclusion to avoid over-strong mechanism claims.
